title: Harness
description: A harness is the agent runtime the factory drives, such as a coding agent under a shared factory process.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A harness is the agent runtime the factory drives. In you-agent-factory, the factory is the workflow system that keeps work persistent; a harness is the coding agent or agent command-line interface that actually runs that work under the factory process.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Knowing which harness runs factory work helps you choose or recognize the agent runtime that executes a goal, and understand why the same factory can target different harnesses.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: When you start a named run, the factory can dispatch that work to a Codex-backed or Cursor-backed agent runtime. That runtime is the harness: it is the agent environment that carries out the steps the factory tracks.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: A harness is not the factory itself. The factory is the workflow system; the harness is the agent runtime it drives. A harness is also not a worker: a worker is a configured work executor declared in factory config, while the harness is the agent runtime that executes agent-loop work. Choosing a runner or model provider is not the same as defining what a harness is—those settings select how a runtime is reached, not the harness concept itself.
